title: Transport
shortSummary: Buy tickets and use Germany's excellent trains, trams, and buses with confidence.
culturalNote: Germany's public transport is dense, punctual, and widely used. Cities combine the U-Bahn (underground), S-Bahn (suburban rail), Straßenbahn (tram), and buses under one ticket system, and long distances run on the fast ICE trains of the Deutsche Bahn. Always validate (entwerten) your ticket where required, because ticket inspectors do check and fines are steep.

keyPhrases.english: When does the next train leave?

grammar.explanation: To say how you travel, use 'mit' plus the dative: mit dem Bus, mit der Bahn, mit dem Auto. 'mit' always takes the dative.
grammar.commonMistake: Using the accusative: it is 'mit dem Bus', not 'mit den Bus'.

grammar.explanation: Use 'fahren' when a vehicle is involved (to drive, ride, travel) and 'gehen' only for going on foot. 'Ich fahre nach Berlin' (by some vehicle), 'Ich gehe zum Park' (walking).

examples.de: Ich fahre mit dem Bus zur Arbeit.
examples.en: I take the bus to work.

grammar.commonMistake: Using 'gehen' for travelling by vehicle. 'Ich gehe nach Berlin' wrongly suggests you walk there.

dialogue.setup: A traveller buys a train ticket at the counter of a German station.

lines.speaker: Reisende
lines.german: Guten Tag, einmal nach München, bitte.
lines.english: Hello, one ticket to Munich, please.

lines.speaker: Reisende
lines.german: Hin und zurück, bitte. Muss ich umsteigen?
lines.english: Return, please. Do I have to change?

lines.speaker: Schalter
lines.german: Ja, einmal in Nürnberg. Der Zug fährt um zehn Uhr.
lines.english: Yes, once in Nuremberg. The train leaves at ten o'clock.

lines.speaker: Schalter
lines.german: Von Gleis vier. Das macht neunzig Euro.
lines.english: From platform four. That's ninety euros.

lines.speaker: Reisende
lines.german: Danke schön. Gute Reise mir selbst!
lines.english: Thank you. Safe travels to me!

quiz.question: How do you say 'I travel by train'?
quiz.explanation: fahren plus mit + dative (dem Zug) for travel by vehicle.

quiz.question: Which means 'return ticket'?
quiz.explanation: hin und zurück means 'there and back', a return.

quiz.question: Ich fahre mit _____ Bus. (masculine dative)
quiz.explanation: mit takes the dative, and der becomes dem.

quiz.question: Which verb means to go on foot?
quiz.explanation: gehen is for walking; fahren is for vehicles.

quiz.question: Arrange: nächste / wann / der / fährt / Zug
quiz.explanation: Question word wann, verb fährt second, then the subject phrase.
